meta.description: Ayurvedic remedies knowledge base sourced from Charaka Samhita and Sushruta Samhita
meta.disclaimer: For informational/educational purposes only. Not a substitute for medical advice.

fatty_liver_grade1.description: Mild hepatic steatosis — early Yakrit Roga with Kapha-Pitta vitiation

remedies.name: Arogyavardhini Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation: purified metals and herbs ground into tablet form per Rasa Ratna Samucchaya guidelines
remedies.dosage: 1-2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with lukewarm water, before meals
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya, Ch. 20; widely referenced in Charaka Samhita Chikitsa Sthana
remedies.mechanism: Katuki (Picrorhiza kurroa) provides hepatoprotective kutkin; Triphala supports bile flow; Guggulu reduces lipid accumulation in hepatocytes

remedies.name: Kumaryasava
remedies.preparation: Asava preparation: herbs fermented with jaggery in earthen pot for 30 days
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 13 (Panduroga Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Aloe vera (Kumari) supports hepatocyte regeneration; self-generated alcohol acts as bio-enhancer for herb absorption; iron content supports hemoglobin

remedies.name: Katuki Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried in shade and powdered finely; sieved through cloth
remedies.dosage: 1-3 g with honey or warm water, twice daily before meals
remedies.mechanism: Kutkin and picroside compounds provide hepatoprotective, cholagogue, and anti-inflammatory effects; reduces Pitta vitiation in Yakrit

lifestyle: Wake before sunrise (Brahma Muhurta) for optimal liver metabolism
Avoid daytime sleep (Divaswapna) — aggravates Kapha in liver
Light dinner before sunset; allow 3-hour gap before sleep
Brisk walking for 30-45 minutes daily
yoga_asanas: Ardha Matsyendrasana (Half Spinal Twist) — massages liver and spleen
Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose) — stretches abdominal muscles, aids liver
Gomukhasana (Cow Face Pose) — stimulates kidneys and liver
pranayama: Kapalabhati (Skull Shining Breath) — 3 rounds of 30 strokes
Bhastrika Pranayama (Bellows Breath) — 2 rounds of 20 strokes
dietary_principles: Favour Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa to pacify Kapha-Pitta
Include bitter gourd (Karavellaka), turmeric (Haridra), and neem leaves
Avoid Snigdha (oily), Guru (heavy) foods — no fried items
Use old barley (Yava) and green gram (Mudga) as staple grains/pulses
Drink warm water infused with cumin (Jeeraka) throughout the day
contraindications: Avoid Arogyavardhini Vati during pregnancy and lactation
Do not use metal-based Bhasmas without practitioner supervision
Discontinue if gastric irritation occurs

fatty_liver_grade2.description: Moderate hepatic steatosis — progressive Yakrit Roga with significant Kapha-Pitta imbalance

remedies.name: Arogyavardhini Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation per Rasa Ratna Samucchaya
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with lukewarm water, before meals
remedies.mechanism: Hepatoprotective Katuki with Guggulu for lipid reduction; Triphala for bile flow support

remedies.name: Punarnava Mandura
remedies.preparation: Mandura Bhasma processed with Punarnava Kwatha (decoction); made into tablets
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with buttermilk
remedies.mechanism: Punarnava is a potent hepato-renal protective; Mandura Bhasma provides bioavailable iron; Trikatu enhances bioavailability

remedies.name: Rohitakarishta
remedies.preparation: Arishta fermented preparation — herbs boiled in water and fermented with jaggery for 30 days
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Rohitaka bark is specific to Yakrit-Pliha Roga (hepato-splenic disease); reduces hepatic congestion and Kapha accumulation

remedies.name: Katuki Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried in shade and powdered finely
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with honey, twice daily before meals
remedies.mechanism: Kutkin provides direct hepatoprotection; picroside-I and II reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in liver

lifestyle: Strictly avoid daytime sleep and late nights
Engage in moderate Vyayama (exercise) daily — walking, swimming
Practice Langhana (therapeutic fasting) once weekly under guidance
Avoid sedentary lifestyle and prolonged sitting
yoga_asanas: Naukasana (Boat Pose) — strengthens core and liver region
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 3 rounds of 50 strokes
Bhastrika Pranayama — 3 rounds of 20 strokes
Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril) — 10 minutes for Pitta balance
dietary_principles: Strict Tikta-Kashaya (bitter-astringent) dominant diet
Old rice (Purana Shali), barley (Yava), and green gram (Mudga) as staples
Absolutely no alcohol, fried foods, refined sugars
Include Patola (pointed gourd), Karavellaka (bitter gourd), Nimba (neem) daily
Cook with Haridra (turmeric) and Jeeraka (cumin) in every meal
contraindications: Avoid aggressive Panchakarma without clinical supervision
Rohitakarishta contains self-generated alcohol — use with caution in liver disease
Monitor liver function tests regularly alongside Ayurvedic treatment

fatty_liver_grade3.description: Severe hepatic steatosis — advanced Yakrit Roga requiring intensive management

remedies.name: Arogyavardhini Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation per Rasa Ratna Samucchaya
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) three times daily under practitioner supervision
remedies.mechanism: Maximum hepatoprotective action through synergy of Katuki, Guggulu, and mineral Bhasmas

remedies.name: Punarnava Mandura
remedies.preparation: Mandura Bhasma processed with Punarnava decoction; tablet form
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with Takra (buttermilk)
remedies.mechanism: Punarnava is a potent hepato-renal protective; addresses Shotha (swelling) and Pandutva (pallor)

remedies.name: Bhumyamalaki Churna
remedies.preparation: Whole plant dried, powdered, and sieved
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water, twice daily
remedies.source: Sushruta Samhita, Sutra Sthana; Bhavaprakasha Nighantu
remedies.mechanism: Phyllanthin and hypophyllanthin provide potent hepatoprotection; reduces HBsAg in studies; anti-fibrotic activity

lifestyle: Mandatory lifestyle overhaul — strict Pathya (regimen) adherence
Avoid all Apathya (unwholesome) foods and habits
Regular medical monitoring alongside Ayurvedic treatment
Consider Panchakarma under experienced Vaidya supervision
yoga_asanas: Supta Baddha Konasana (Reclining Bound Angle) — gentle abdominal opening
pranayama: Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril Breathing) — 15 minutes twice daily
Sheetali Pranayama (Cooling Breath) — to pacify aggravated Pitta
dietary_principles: Exclusively Laghu (light) and Sukha Pachya (easily digestible) foods
Mudga Yusha (green gram soup) as primary protein source
No oil/ghee except minimal coconut oil for cooking
Avoid all processed, packaged, and fermented foods
Warm water with lemon and turmeric on waking
contraindications: Severe fatty liver requires concurrent allopathic monitoring
Avoid all Asava/Arishta (fermented preparations) in Grade 3
Do not attempt Virechana (purgation) without specialist supervision
Metal-based Bhasmas require close monitoring of liver enzymes

hepatomegaly.description: Enlarged liver (Yakrit Vriddhi) — predominantly Pitta aggravation with Kapha obstruction

remedies.name: Rohitakarishta
remedies.preparation: Arishta fermented preparation per Charaka method
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Rohitaka is the principal drug for Yakrit-Pliha Roga; reduces hepatic congestion

remedies.name: Punarnava Mandura
remedies.preparation: Classical tablet preparation with iron calx
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with buttermilk
remedies.mechanism: Punarnava reduces Shotha (swelling/oedema); Mandura Bhasma addresses iron deficiency commonly seen

lifestyle: Avoid heavy meals — eat small, frequent, warm meals
Avoid alcohol and hepatotoxic substances completely
Practice stress management — chronic stress aggravates Pitta
yoga_asanas: Vajrasana (Thunderbolt Pose) after meals — aids digestion
pranayama: Sheetali Pranayama — cooling breath to reduce Pitta
Anulom Vilom — 10 minutes for balance
dietary_principles: Favour Tikta Rasa (bitter taste) — Patola, Karavellaka, Guduchi
Light, warm, freshly cooked meals only
Avoid spicy, sour, and fermented foods (Pitta-aggravating)
contraindications: Investigate underlying cause (hepatitis, congestion) before starting treatment
Avoid Arishta preparations if liver function is severely compromised

kidney_stones.description: Renal calculi (Ashmari) — Vata dries Kapha forming stone-like concretions in Mutravaha Srotas

remedies.name: Gokshuradi Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu processed with Gokshura decoction; rolled into pills
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.source: Sushruta Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 7 (Ashmari Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Gokshura is Ashmarighna (stone-destroying); saponins increase urine output and flush micro-calculi; Guggulu is anti-inflammatory

remedies.name: Pashanabheda Churna
remedies.preparation: Rhizome dried and powdered
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with coconut water or warm water, twice daily
remedies.source: Sushruta Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 7; Bhavaprakasha Nighantu
remedies.mechanism: Name literally means 'stone-breaker'; bergenin compound demonstrates litholytic activity and inhibits calcium oxalate crystallization

remedies.name: Varunadi Kwatha
remedies.preparation: Coarse powder of herbs; boil 1 part powder in 16 parts water, reduce to 1/4
remedies.dosage: 40-50 ml decoction, twice daily on empty stomach
remedies.mechanism: Varuna bark contains lupeol which inhibits oxalate synthesis and deposition; Gokshura and Punarnava flush the urinary tract

lifestyle: Drink minimum 3 litres of warm water daily (Ushnodaka Pana)
Avoid prolonged sitting — walk after meals
Reduce salt intake and oxalate-rich foods
pranayama: Anulom Vilom — 10 minutes for Vata pacification
Ujjayi Pranayama — calming, reduces Vata
dietary_principles: Hydration is paramount — barley water (Yavagu) is excellent
Favour Kulitha (horse gram) soup for litholytic action
Avoid high-oxalate foods: spinach, tomato, rhubarb
Reduce dairy and excess calcium if calcium oxalate type
Include coconut water, cucumber, and watermelon regularly
contraindications: Stones >10 mm may require surgical intervention — do not delay
Obstructive uropathy is a medical emergency — seek immediate care
Monitor renal function during treatment

gallstones.description: Cholelithiasis (Pittashmari) — Pitta aggravation causes bile stasis; Kapha solidifies it

remedies.name: Arogyavardhini Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation per Rasa Ratna Samucchaya
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water after meals
remedies.mechanism: Katuki stimulates bile flow (cholagogue); prevents bile stasis; Triphala promotes GI motility

remedies.name: Tamra Bhasma
remedies.preparation: Copper purified through repeated Shodhana; incinerated with herbal juices per Rasa Shastra
remedies.dosage: 15-30 mg with honey, once daily under strict supervision
remedies.mechanism: Tamra Bhasma is considered specific for Pittashmari; micro-dose copper supports biliary function and prevents cholesterol crystallization

lifestyle: Avoid large, fatty meals — eat small, frequent meals
Do not skip meals — prolonged fasting aggravates bile stasis
Moderate exercise daily — 30-minute brisk walk
pranayama: Sheetali Pranayama — cooling, reduces Pitta
Kapalabhati — gentle rounds to stimulate digestion
dietary_principles: Strictly low-fat diet — avoid ghee, butter, fried foods
Include apple, pear, and beetroot for natural cholagogue effect
Warm lemon water on waking to stimulate bile flow
Favour Tikta Rasa (bitter) herbs and vegetables
contraindications: Symptomatic gallstones with recurrent colic may require cholecystectomy
Tamra Bhasma must be properly prepared — impure copper is toxic
Do not attempt gallbladder flush without medical supervision

splenomegaly.description: Enlarged spleen (Pliha Vriddhi) — Pitta imbalance with Rakta Dhatu involvement

remedies.name: Rohitakarishta
remedies.preparation: Arishta fermentation as per classical method
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Rohitaka is Yakrit-Pliha Hara (hepato-splenic specific); reduces splenic congestion

remedies.name: Pippalyasava
remedies.preparation: Asava fermentation with Pippali as primary herb
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Pippali kindles Agni (digestive fire) and reduces splenic Kapha; piperine enhances absorption of co-administered herbs

lifestyle: Avoid contact sports — enlarged spleen is vulnerable to rupture
Anti-inflammatory diet and lifestyle
Adequate rest with moderate non-impact exercise
pranayama: Anulom Vilom — balancing, gentle
Bhramari (Bee Breathing) — calming effect
dietary_principles: Light, easily digestible meals — Mudga Yusha (green gram soup), old rice
Include pomegranate (Dadima) — specific for Raktapitta and Pliha conditions
Avoid heavy, cold, and Kapha-aggravating foods
Turmeric milk (Haridra Ksheera) daily
contraindications: Investigate underlying cause (infection, haematological) before treatment
Avoid vigorous abdominal exercises and yoga
Splenomegaly with cytopenias requires haematologist evaluation

diabetes.description: Prameha/Madhumeha — initial Kapha provocation progresses through Pitta to Vata stage

remedies.name: Chandraprabha Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical multi-herb tablet prepared per Sharangdhara Samhita
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.mechanism: Acts on Mutravaha Srotas; Shilajit contains fulvic acid with hypoglycaemic activity; Guggulu improves insulin sensitivity

remedies.name: Nishamalaki Churna
remedies.preparation: Equal parts of Haridra and Amalaki churna mixed
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water or honey, twice daily before meals
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 6 (Prameha Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Curcumin improves insulin sensitivity and reduces inflammation; Amalaki is rich in vitamin C and chromium, supports glucose metabolism

remedies.name: Shilajit Vati
remedies.preparation: Purified Shilajit processed with Triphala decoction and formed into tablets
remedies.dosage: 250-500 mg twice daily with warm milk or water
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 6; Sushruta Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 11
remedies.mechanism: Fulvic and humic acids improve glucose uptake; traditionally called 'conqueror of mountains and destroyer of weakness'

lifestyle: Regular Vyayama (exercise) — minimum 45 minutes daily
Avoid daytime sleep; sleep by 10 PM
Padabhyanga (foot massage with warm sesame oil) daily for neuropathy prevention
Regular monitoring of blood glucose alongside Ayurvedic treatment
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 5 rounds of 30 strokes (stimulates pancreas)
Bhastrika — 3 rounds of 20 strokes
Anulom Vilom — 15 minutes for autonomic balance
dietary_principles: Yava (barley) is the grain of choice for Prameha — use as staple
Mudga (green gram) is the best pulse for diabetics per Charaka
Include Methi (fenugreek) seeds soaked overnight — 1 tsp daily
Bitter gourd (Karavellaka) juice — 30 ml on empty stomach
Avoid all Madhura Rasa (sweet) foods, especially refined sugars
Jambu (Jamun/Indian blackberry) seeds powder — 3 g with water
contraindications: Do not replace insulin or OHA with Ayurvedic medicines without medical guidance
Shilajit is contraindicated in hyperuricemia
Monitor HbA1c every 3 months to assess treatment efficacy

prediabetes.description: Early Prameha — predominant Kapha vitiation with impaired Agni (digestive fire)

remedies.name: Nishamalaki Churna
remedies.preparation: Equal parts of Haridra and Amalaki churna mixed
remedies.dosage: 3 g with warm water, twice daily before meals
remedies.mechanism: Curcumin and amla synergistically improve insulin sensitivity and reduce oxidative stress

remedies.name: Triphala Churna
remedies.preparation: Equal parts dried fruits powdered and mixed
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water at bedtime
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana, Ch. 25; Sushruta Samhita, Sutra Sthana, Ch. 38
remedies.mechanism: Regulates carbohydrate metabolism; gallic acid and chebulagic acid improve insulin signalling; powerful antioxidant

lifestyle: Increase daily physical activity — brisk walking 45 minutes
Weight reduction if overweight — Kapha-pacifying regimen
Avoid sedentary lifestyle and excessive sleep
Early dinner (before 7 PM) with light foods
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 3 rounds of 30 strokes
Bhastrika — 2 rounds of 20 strokes
dietary_principles: Replace white rice with barley (Yava) and millets
Include fenugreek (Methi) and bitter gourd regularly
Avoid Guru (heavy), Snigdha (oily), and Madhura (sweet) dominant foods
Favour Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) tastes
contraindications: Prediabetes may reverse with lifestyle alone — avoid over-medication
Monitor fasting glucose monthly to track progression

dyslipidemia.description: Medoroga / Dhamanipratichaya — excess Meda Dhatu (fat tissue) with Kapha vitiation obstructing Srotas (channels)

remedies.name: Medohar Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu processed with herb decoctions; formed into tablets
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water after meals
remedies.source: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Sthaulya Rogadhikara; Charaka Samhita reference
remedies.mechanism: Guggulsterones (Z and E) demonstrated to reduce LDL and increase HDL; Triphala reduces intestinal cholesterol absorption

remedies.name: Arjuna Ksheerapaka
remedies.preparation: 1 tsp Arjuna bark powder boiled in 1 cup milk + 1 cup water, reduced to 1 cup
remedies.dosage: 1 cup Arjuna Ksheerapaka daily on empty stomach, morning
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana; Sushruta Samhita — Arjuna is Hridya (cardioprotective)
remedies.mechanism: Arjunic acid and arjungenin strengthen cardiac muscle; bark tannins reduce LDL oxidation; improves HDL/LDL ratio

lifestyle: Regular aerobic exercise — 45-60 minutes, 5 days a week
Dry powder massage (Udvartana) with Triphala or Kolkulaththa churna
Avoid daytime sleep — strongly Kapha-aggravating
Eat warm, freshly cooked meals; avoid cold and heavy foods
yoga_asanas: Surya Namaskar — 12+ rounds for cardiovascular benefit
Sarvangasana (Shoulder Stand) — stimulates thyroid, improves metabolism
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 5 rounds of 50 strokes (metabolic activation)
Bhastrika — 3 rounds of 30 strokes
Surya Bhedana (Right Nostril Breathing) — activating
dietary_principles: Favour Katu (pungent), Tikta (bitter), Kashaya (astringent) tastes
Use honey (Madhu) as sweetener — scrapes Kapha per Charaka
Include garlic (Lashuna), ginger (Shunthi), and turmeric daily
Replace ghee with mustard oil or minimal sesame oil
Old barley (Yava) and millets as carbohydrate sources
contraindications: Honey should never be heated — becomes toxic per Ayurveda
Severe dyslipidemia with CAD history requires statin therapy — do not replace
Guggulu may interact with thyroid medications and anticoagulants

hypothyroidism.description: Kapha dominance in Agni leading to Dhatvagnimandya (reduced tissue metabolism)

remedies.name: Kanchanara Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Kanchanara bark decoction used to process Guggulu; tablet form
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.mechanism: Kanchanara is specific for Galaganda (goitre/thyroid conditions); stimulates thyroid function; Guggulu has known thyroid-stimulating activity via T4 to T3 conversion

remedies.name: Ashwagandha Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried, powdered, and sieved
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm milk at bedtime
remedies.mechanism: Withanolides stimulate thyroid hormone secretion; adaptogenic — reduces cortisol which suppresses TSH; improves T4 levels in subclinical hypothyroidism

lifestyle: Regular morning exercise — essential for Kapha balance
Avoid cold foods and cold environments
Ensure adequate iodine from natural sources (sea salt, seaweed)
yoga_asanas: Simhasana (Lion Pose) — stimulates throat and thyroid
pranayama: Ujjayi Pranayama (Ocean Breath) — vibration stimulates thyroid
Kapalabhati — metabolic activation
dietary_principles: Avoid raw goitrogenic vegetables (cabbage, cauliflower, broccoli) — cook them well
Ensure iodised salt usage; include sea fish if non-vegetarian
Favour warm, light, Katu (pungent) foods
Include coconut oil — medium-chain triglycerides support thyroid
contraindications: Do not discontinue Levothyroxine for Ayurvedic treatment
Guggulu affects thyroid — monitor TSH closely if on medication
Sarvangasana contraindicated in cervical spondylosis and hypertension

hyperuricemia.description: Vatarakta — Vata carrying aggravated Rakta Dhatu (blood); uric acid is Ama in Rakta

remedies.name: Kaishor Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu processed with decoction of above herbs; formed into tablets
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.source: Sharangdhara Samhita; indicated in Vatarakta per Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 29
remedies.mechanism: Guduchi is anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory; Guggulu reduces swelling; Triphala detoxifies Rakta Dhatu through gentle purgation

remedies.name: Punarnava Churna
remedies.preparation: Whole plant dried and powdered
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water, twice daily
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana; Bhavaprakasha Nighantu
remedies.mechanism: Potent diuretic and anti-inflammatory; increases uric acid excretion through kidneys; reduces Shotha (swelling)

lifestyle: Hydration — minimum 3 litres water daily to flush uric acid
Avoid alcohol completely — especially beer
Maintain healthy body weight
Avoid fasting — it increases uric acid through ketosis
pranayama: Anulom Vilom — Vata pacification
Ujjayi Pranayama — calming
dietary_principles: Strictly avoid high-purine foods: organ meats, red meat, shellfish
Include cherry (shown to reduce uric acid), cucumber, and celery
Milk and low-fat dairy are protective in hyperuricemia
Favour vegetarian diet — Mudga (green gram), Yava (barley)
Avoid Vidahi (irritating) and Abhishyandi (channel-blocking) foods
contraindications: Acute gouty arthritis attack — use Colchicine/NSAIDs; Ayurveda as adjunct
Guggulu may interact with anticoagulants
Tophi formation requires allopathic urate-lowering therapy

vitamin_d_deficiency.description: Asthi-Kshaya tendency — Vata aggravation depleting Asthi Dhatu (bone tissue)

remedies.name: Ashwagandha Ksheerapaka
remedies.preparation: 1 tsp Ashwagandha powder boiled in 1 cup milk + 1 cup water, reduced to 1 cup
remedies.dosage: 1 cup at bedtime daily
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana (Rasayana); Ashtanga Hridaya
remedies.mechanism: Ashwagandha promotes Asthi Dhatu formation; withanolides enhance calcium absorption; adaptogenic — reduces cortisol that depletes bone

remedies.name: Praval Pishti
remedies.preparation: Coral ground with rose water repeatedly until superfine; classical Pishti preparation
remedies.dosage: 250-500 mg with honey or warm milk, once daily
remedies.source: Rasa Tarangini; traditional Rasa Shastra text
remedies.mechanism: Natural calcium carbonate in highly bioavailable form; cooling (Sheeta Virya) — pacifies Pitta while nourishing Asthi Dhatu

lifestyle: Morning sunlight exposure (Surya Atapa Sevana) — 20-30 minutes before 10 AM
Sesame oil Abhyanga (self-massage) — nourishes Asthi Dhatu
Weight-bearing exercise for bone health
yoga_asanas: Surya Namaskar — performed in morning sun
Vrikshasana (Tree Pose) — balance and bone loading
pranayama: Surya Bhedana (Right Nostril Breathing) — warming, Vata-pacifying
Anulom Vilom — 10 minutes
dietary_principles: Sesame seeds (Tila) are best for Asthi Dhatu per Charaka
Include cow milk (Ksheera), ghee (Ghrita) for calcium and fat-soluble vitamin absorption
Almond (Vatada) milk soaked overnight and ground
Avoid excess Vata-aggravating foods: raw salads, dry foods, carbonated drinks
contraindications: Severe Vitamin D deficiency (<10 ng/mL) requires pharmaceutical supplementation
Coral-based preparations must be properly sourced and prepared
Monitor serum calcium when supplementing

liver_stress.description: Early Yakrit Dushti — Pitta aggravation causing hepatic stress without structural damage

remedies.name: Katuki Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried and powdered
remedies.dosage: 1-3 g with honey, twice daily
remedies.mechanism: Premier hepatoprotective herb in Ayurveda; kutkin protects hepatocytes from oxidative damage

remedies.name: Kumaryasava
remedies.preparation: Asava fermentation per classical method
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Aloe vera supports hepatocyte regeneration and reduces liver inflammation

lifestyle: Eliminate or reduce alcohol consumption
Reduce processed food intake
Include liver-supportive foods daily
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 3 rounds of 30 strokes
Sheetali Pranayama — if Pitta is prominent
dietary_principles: Include Haridra (turmeric), Katuki, and Bhumyamalaki as regular supplements
Bitter vegetables: Karavellaka (bitter gourd), Patola (pointed gourd)
Avoid all hepatotoxins — alcohol, paracetamol excess, fried foods
contraindications: Persistent elevated liver enzymes need medical workup
Rule out viral hepatitis before attributing to lifestyle alone

insulin_resistance.description: Meda-Dhatvagni Mandya — impaired fat tissue metabolism with Kapha blocking insulin receptors

remedies.name: Chandraprabha Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical multi-herb preparation
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.mechanism: Shilajit fulvic acid improves insulin sensitivity; Guggulu addresses lipid component of insulin resistance

remedies.name: Nishamalaki Churna
remedies.preparation: Equal parts mixed
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water, twice daily
remedies.mechanism: Curcumin is a potent insulin sensitizer; Amalaki provides chromium and antioxidant support

lifestyle: Daily vigorous exercise — at least 45 minutes
Weight reduction through Kapha-pacifying regimen
Avoid sedentary habits and excessive sleep
pranayama: Kapalabhati — 5 rounds of 30 strokes
Bhastrika — 3 rounds
dietary_principles: Low glycaemic index foods: barley, millets, green gram
Include Methi (fenugreek), Jambu seeds, turmeric
Avoid all refined carbohydrates and sugars
Favour Tikta and Katu Rasa — bitter and pungent tastes
contraindications: If HbA1c > 6.5%, concurrent allopathic management needed
Monitor fasting insulin and glucose regularly

kidney_impairment.description: Mutravaha Srotas Dushti — impaired urinary channel function with Vata-Pitta vitiation

remedies.name: Gokshuradi Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu processed with Gokshura decoction
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with warm water
remedies.mechanism: Gokshura is Mutrala (diuretic) and Brimhana (nourishing); supports renal function without depleting electrolytes

remedies.name: Punarnava Churna
remedies.preparation: Whole plant dried and powdered
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water, twice daily
remedies.mechanism: Name means 'one who renews the body'; punarnavine is nephroprotective and diuretic

lifestyle: Adequate but not excessive hydration — adjust per GFR
Avoid nephrotoxic substances — NSAIDs, excess protein
Regular monitoring of renal function
pranayama: Anulom Vilom — gentle, balancing
Bhramari — calming
dietary_principles: Moderate protein intake per renal function
Limit sodium, potassium, phosphorus as per stage of disease
Favour Gokshura-infused water throughout the day
Include Varuna bark decoction for renal support
contraindications: CKD Stage 4-5 requires nephrologist management — Ayurveda is adjunctive
Potassium-rich Ayurvedic herbs may be dangerous in advanced CKD
Avoid Bhasma (metal) preparations if GFR < 30

iron_deficiency.description: Panduroga — primarily Pitta-Rakta depletion with secondary Vata aggravation

remedies.name: Lohasava
remedies.preparation: Asava fermentation with iron Bhasma for enhanced absorption
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 16 (Panduroga Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Nano-sized iron from Bhasma is highly bioavailable; Dhataki-fermented medium enhances absorption; Trikatu improves iron uptake

remedies.name: Punarnava Mandura
remedies.preparation: Iron calx processed with Punarnava decoction; tablet form
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with buttermilk or orange juice
remedies.mechanism: Mandura Bhasma provides bioavailable iron; Punarnava addresses oedema seen in chronic anaemia; Trikatu enhances absorption

remedies.name: Dhatri Lauh
remedies.preparation: Loha Bhasma triturated with Amalaki juice multiple times; dried and tableted
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with honey
remedies.mechanism: Amalaki vitamin C dramatically enhances iron absorption; Yashti Madhu soothes GI tract preventing iron-related gastric irritation

lifestyle: Cook in iron vessels (Loha Patra) — traditional iron supplementation
Avoid tea and coffee with meals — tannins inhibit iron absorption
Include vitamin C-rich foods with iron-rich meals
Adequate rest — avoid overexertion in moderate-severe anaemia
yoga_asanas: Viparita Karani (Legs Up the Wall) — gentle inversion for circulation
pranayama: Anulom Vilom — improves oxygenation
Deep abdominal breathing — enhances oxygen carrying capacity utilisation
dietary_principles: Dates (Kharjura), pomegranate (Dadima), and jaggery (Guda) are Raktavardhaka (blood builders)
Include green leafy vegetables — especially Palak (spinach) and Methi
Soak grains and legumes to reduce phytates that block iron
Black sesame seeds (Krishna Tila) with honey — traditional Rakta Dhatu builder
contraindications: Severe anaemia (Hb < 7 g/dL) requires blood transfusion — do not delay
Iron Bhasmas in excess can cause iron overload — periodic CBC monitoring needed
Investigate cause of anaemia (menorrhagia, GI bleeding) alongside treatment

inflammation.description: Amavata / Daha — Pitta aggravation with Ama (toxin) accumulation in Dhatus

remedies.name: Guggulu Tiktaka Ghrita
remedies.preparation: Ghrita (medicated ghee) prepared by cooking herbs in ghee per Sneha Kalpana
remedies.dosage: 5-10 ml with warm water, twice daily before meals
remedies.mechanism: Ghrita is the best Pitta-shamaka; bitter herbs reduce inflammation; Guduchi (Tinospora cordifolia) is a potent immunomodulator

remedies.name: Amritarishta
remedies.preparation: Arishta fermentation with Guduchi as primary herb
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Guduchi is Amrita (divine nectar); tinosporin and berberine have potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activity

lifestyle: Stress management — chronic stress perpetuates Pitta inflammation
Regular Abhyanga (oil massage) with cooling oils (coconut, Chandanadi)
Avoid excessive sun exposure and heat
Adequate sleep — 7-8 hours; sleep by 10 PM (Pitta time begins)
yoga_asanas: Gentle Hatha Yoga — avoid overly vigorous practice
pranayama: Sheetali Pranayama — cooling breath, reduces Pitta
Sheetkari Pranayama — cooling through teeth
Anulom Vilom — 15 minutes for balance
dietary_principles: Anti-inflammatory diet: Tikta (bitter) and Madhura (sweet) Rasa dominant
Include Guduchi Satva, Amalaki, turmeric daily
Avoid Vidahi (foods causing burning) — chillies, fermented foods, alcohol
Cooling foods: coconut, cucumber, coriander, fennel
Ghee is anti-inflammatory per Ayurveda — 1-2 tsp daily with meals
contraindications: Chronic inflammatory conditions (RA, IBD) need concurrent allopathic management
Ghrita (ghee) preparations avoided in severe dyslipidemia and obesity
Guduchi may lower blood sugar — monitor in diabetic patients

pancreatic_abnormality.description: Agnyashaya Shotha — acute Pitta aggravation in the seat of Jatharagni

remedies.name: Sutashekhar Rasa
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation; metals purified and incinerated
remedies.dosage: 125-250 mg with honey, twice daily (under strict supervision only)
remedies.mechanism: Shankha Bhasma (conch shell) is antacid; controls Pitta-driven acid secretion; provides calcium carbonate in nano form

lifestyle: Strict Langhana (fasting/light diet) during acute phase
Absolute avoidance of alcohol
Avoid heavy, oily, fried foods permanently
Stress management — Pitta aggravated by anger and stress
yoga_asanas: Only restorative poses during and after acute episodes
pranayama: Sheetali Pranayama — cooling, reduces Pitta
Chandra Bhedana (Left Nostril Breathing) — cooling
dietary_principles: Ultra-low-fat diet — <20 g fat/day
Mudga Yusha (green gram soup) with minimal spice
Old rice (Purana Shali) gruel during recovery
Avoid all pungent, sour, and salty foods
Coconut water for hydration and cooling
contraindications: Acute pancreatitis is a medical emergency — hospitalization required
Rasa Shastra preparations absolutely require expert supervision
Do not use Bhasmas if pancreatic function is severely compromised

fatty_liver.description: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) — Yakrit Roga with Meda Dhatu vitiation

remedies.name: Arogyavardhini Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa Shastra preparation: purified metals and herbs ground into tablet form per Rasa Ratna Samucchaya guidelines
remedies.dosage: 1-2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with lukewarm water, before meals
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya, Ch. 20; widely referenced in Charaka Samhita Chikitsa Sthana
remedies.mechanism: Katuki (Picrorhiza kurroa) provides hepatoprotective kutkin; Triphala supports bile flow; Guggulu reduces lipid accumulation in hepatocytes

remedies.name: Kumaryasava
remedies.preparation: Asava preparation: herbs fermented with jaggery in earthen pot for 30 days
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water, twice daily after meals
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 13 (Panduroga Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Aloe vera (Kumari) supports hepatocyte regeneration; self-generated alcohol acts as bio-enhancer for herb absorption; iron content supports hemoglobin

remedies.name: Katuki Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried in shade and powdered finely; sieved through cloth
remedies.dosage: 1-3 g with honey or warm water, twice daily before meals
remedies.mechanism: Kutkin and picroside compounds provide hepatoprotective, cholagogue, and anti-inflammatory effects; reduces Pitta vitiation in Yakrit

lifestyle: Wake before sunrise (Brahma Muhurta) for optimal liver metabolism
Avoid daytime sleep (Divaswapna) — aggravates Kapha in liver
Light dinner before sunset; allow 3-hour gap before sleep
Brisk walking for 30-45 minutes daily
yoga_asanas: Ardha Matsyendrasana (Half Spinal Twist) — massages liver and spleen
Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose) — stretches abdominal muscles, aids liver
Gomukhasana (Cow Face Pose) — stimulates kidneys and liver
pranayama: Kapalabhati (Skull Shining Breath) — 3 rounds of 30 strokes
Bhastrika Pranayama (Bellows Breath) — 2 rounds of 20 strokes
dietary_principles: Favour Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa to pacify Kapha-Pitta
Include bitter gourd (Karavellaka), turmeric (Haridra), and neem leaves
Avoid Snigdha (oily), Guru (heavy) foods — no fried items
Use old barley (Yava) and green gram (Mudga) as staple grains/pulses
Drink warm water infused with cumin (Jeeraka) throughout the day
contraindications: Avoid Arogyavardhini Vati during pregnancy and lactation
Do not use metal-based Bhasmas without practitioner supervision
Discontinue if gastric irritation occurs

hyperthyroidism.description: Atisweda Roga — excessive metabolic fire (Agni) with Vata-Pitta vitiation

remedies.name: Shatavari Ghrita
remedies.preparation: Shatavari root boiled in ghee with milk as liquid medium until ghee remains
remedies.dosage: 1 teaspoon twice daily with warm milk
remedies.mechanism: Shatavari is cooling (Sheeta Virya) and nourishing; ghee medium reduces excess Pitta and Vata; balances thyroid hyperactivity

remedies.name: Chandanadi Vati
remedies.preparation: Fine powders mixed with rose water, formed into tablets, dried in shade
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with cool water
remedies.mechanism: Cooling herbs reduce Pitta-driven hypermetabolism; Chandana calms nervous system

remedies.name: Praval Pishti
remedies.preparation: Coral calcium triturated with rose water for 21 days
remedies.dosage: 250-500 mg twice daily with honey
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya, referenced in Charaka Samhita context
remedies.mechanism: Calcium-rich coral supports bones weakened by hyperthyroidism; cooling Pitta-pacifying properties

lifestyle: Avoid excessive heat and sun exposure
Regular oil massage (Abhyanga) with coconut oil
Meditation and Yoga Nidra for calming overactive mind
Adequate rest — avoid overexertion
pranayama: Sheetali Pranayama (Cooling Breath) — cools excess Pitta
Chandra Bhedana (Left Nostril Breathing) — cooling effect
Bhramari (Humming Bee Breath) — calms nervous system
dietary_principles: Favour cool, sweet, bitter foods (Sheeta, Madhura, Tikta Rasa)
Increase dairy, coconut, sweet fruits
Avoid spicy, sour, fermented foods
Drink coconut water and cool herbal teas
contraindications: Avoid Kapalabhati and Bhastrika — too heating
Do not use Shilajit — increases metabolism further

anemia.description: Pandu Roga — pallor disease with Rasa-Rakta Dhatu depletion

remedies.name: Lohasava
remedies.preparation: Iron calx with herbs fermented in jaggery solution for 30 days
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water after meals, twice daily
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 16 (Panduroga Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Bio-available iron from Loha Bhasma; Triphala enhances absorption; self-generated alcohol improves bioavailability

remedies.name: Dhatri Lauha
remedies.preparation: Iron calx triturated with Amalaki juice and licorice, formed into tablets
remedies.dosage: 1-2 tablets twice daily after meals
remedies.mechanism: Vitamin C from Amalaki dramatically enhances iron absorption; licorice protects gastric mucosa

remedies.name: Punarnava Mandura
remedies.preparation: Mandura Bhasma triturated with Punarnava juice, mixed with other herb powders
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets (250 mg each) twice daily with buttermilk
remedies.mechanism: Punarnava is Rasayana for Rakta Dhatu; Mandura Bhasma provides bio-available iron; Triphala supports absorption

lifestyle: Early morning sun exposure (15-20 min) for vitamin D synthesis aiding iron metabolism
Avoid tea/coffee with meals — inhibit iron absorption
Regular moderate exercise to stimulate blood formation
Adequate sleep for Rakta Dhatu regeneration
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana (Alternate Nostril) — balances Prana
Bhastrika (Bellows Breath) — increases oxygen in blood
Deep Abdominal Breathing — 10 minutes daily
dietary_principles: Iron-rich foods: dates, raisins, pomegranate, beetroot, jaggery, green leafy vegetables
Pair iron foods with vitamin C (lemon, amla)
Cook in iron vessels (Loha Patra)
Avoid excess calcium with iron-rich meals
contraindications: Severe anemia (Hb < 7) requires medical intervention first
Do not rely solely on Ayurvedic iron — monitor Hb levels

vitamin_b12_deficiency.description: Vata-driven nerve and blood tissue depletion — Majja and Rakta Dhatu Kshaya

remedies.name: Ashwagandha Churna
remedies.preparation: Root dried, powdered, and sieved; can be mixed with warm milk
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm milk and honey, twice daily
remedies.mechanism: Ashwagandha is Medhya Rasayana — nourishes nerve tissue (Majja Dhatu) affected by B12 deficiency; adaptogenic properties support overall vitality

remedies.name: Saraswatarishta
remedies.preparation: Arishta fermentation of nervine herbs with jaggery for 30 days
remedies.dosage: 15-20 ml with equal water after meals
remedies.mechanism: Brahmi and Ashwagandha provide Medhya (nootropic) support; fermented preparation enhances absorption; supports Majja Dhatu

remedies.name: Bala Ashwagandha Taila (external)
remedies.preparation: Herbs boiled in sesame oil until oil base remains; strained
remedies.dosage: External Abhyanga (massage) — apply to hands, feet, and spine daily
remedies.mechanism: Topical nervine oils penetrate through skin to nourish peripheral nerves; sesame oil is Vata-pacifying

lifestyle: Include fermented foods (traditional buttermilk, kanji) for natural B12
Regular Abhyanga with sesame oil for peripheral neuropathy
Avoid excessive fasting — depletes Dhatus
Ensure adequate dairy/egg intake if not strictly vegan
yoga_asanas: Viparita Karani (Legs Up the Wall) — improves nerve blood flow
Yoga Nidra — deep nervous system restoration
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana (Alternate Nostril) — balances Vata in nervous system
Bhramari (Humming Bee) — calms Vata and supports Majja Dhatu
Deep Breathing with counting — 4:7:8 ratio
dietary_principles: Dairy products (milk, curd, paneer) are primary vegetarian B12 sources in Ayurveda
Fermented preparations (Arishta, Asava) contain naturally produced B12
Ghee — nourishes Majja Dhatu and aids nerve function
Supplementation recommended for strict vegans alongside Ayurvedic support
contraindications: Severe B12 deficiency with neurological symptoms needs medical B12 injections
Do not delay medical treatment for severe cases

electrolyte_imbalance.description: Dhatu Kshaya — tissue depletion affecting mineral balance and fluid regulation

remedies.name: Lavan Bhaskar Churna
remedies.preparation: Five types of salt with carminative herbs ground together and sieved
remedies.dosage: 1-3 g with warm water or buttermilk before meals
remedies.mechanism: Balanced mineral salts restore electrolyte equilibrium; Trikatu enhances Agni for better mineral absorption

remedies.name: Takra (Medicated Buttermilk)
remedies.preparation: Churn curd with water (1:4 ratio), remove butter, add spices
remedies.dosage: 1 glass with meals or between meals, 2-3 times daily
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana, Ch. 27; Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 19
remedies.mechanism: Natural electrolyte replacement; probiotics support mineral absorption; cumin and ginger kindle Agni

lifestyle: Stay hydrated with room-temperature water throughout the day
Avoid excessive sweating without mineral replacement
Regular meal timing to maintain Agni and absorption
Coconut water as natural electrolyte source
yoga_asanas: Viparita Karani (Legs Up the Wall) — improves fluid circulation
Supta Baddha Konasana — calms nervous system
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — balances Prana and ion channels
Deep Abdominal Breathing — calms nervous system
dietary_principles: Include all six Rasas (tastes) in meals for mineral balance
Rock salt (Saindhava) preferred over table salt — richer in minerals
Coconut water, tender coconut, banana, dates for potassium
Adequate dairy for calcium; green leafy vegetables for magnesium
contraindications: Severe electrolyte imbalance with cardiac symptoms requires emergency medical care
Do not self-treat severe hyponatremia or hyperkalemia

proteinuria.description: Prameha-related Mutra Vikara — urinary abnormality with protein loss indicating kidney stress

remedies.name: Gokshuradi Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu-based tablet preparation with Gokshura as primary herb
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with warm water
remedies.mechanism: Gokshura is the premier Mutrala (diuretic) — protects kidney nephrons; Guggulu reduces inflammation; Triphala supports elimination

remedies.name: Chandraprabha Vati
remedies.preparation: Classical tablet preparation per Sharangdhara Samhita methodology
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with warm milk or water
remedies.source: Sharangdhara Samhita, Madhyama Khanda, Ch. 7; referenced in Charaka
remedies.mechanism: Shilajit provides fulvic acid that supports kidney tissue; Chandraprabha herb is Mutravaha Srotas Shodhaka (urinary channel cleanser)

lifestyle: Moderate protein intake — avoid excess in diet
Adequate hydration with plain water
Avoid holding urine (Vegadharana) — Charaka lists this as health-depleting
Regular Gokshura decoction as kidney tonic
yoga_asanas: Viparita Karani — improves renal blood flow
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — balances Apana Vayu governing kidneys
Ujjayi — gentle, calming to nervous system
dietary_principles: Moderate protein — avoid excess meat and dairy
Increase Gokshura tea, barley water, coconut water
Avoid excess salt and processed foods
Light, easily digestible meals to reduce kidney burden
contraindications: Heavy proteinuria (> 3g/day) requires nephrologist evaluation
Do not replace medical management for nephrotic syndrome

folate_deficiency.description: Rakta Dhatu Kshaya — blood tissue depletion affecting cell division and DNA synthesis

remedies.name: Triphala Churna
remedies.preparation: Equal parts of three fruits dried and powdered; sieved fine
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm water at bedtime
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana, Ch. 25; Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 1
remedies.mechanism: Amalaki is among the richest natural sources of vitamin C and folate; Triphala improves gut absorption of nutrients; Rasayana properties rejuvenate Dhatus

remedies.name: Amalaki Rasayana
remedies.preparation: Fresh Amalaki pulp mixed with ghee, honey (unequal quantities), and Pippali powder
remedies.dosage: 1-2 teaspoons daily on empty stomach
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 1 (Rasayana Pada)
remedies.mechanism: Amalaki is Sarvadosha Shamaka (balances all Doshas); provides natural folate and vitamin C; Rasayana for Rakta Dhatu

lifestyle: Include fresh green leafy vegetables daily (Shaka Varga)
Consume citrus fruits for natural folate + vitamin C
Avoid excess alcohol — depletes folate stores
Regular meal timing for optimal nutrient absorption
yoga_asanas: Sarvangasana — improves thyroid and blood formation
Uttanasana (Standing Forward Bend) — blood flow to digestive organs
Paschimottanasana — stimulates digestion and nutrient absorption
pranayama: Bhastrika — increases oxygenation of blood
Nadi Shodhana — balances nutrient distribution through Nadis
dietary_principles: Green leafy vegetables (spinach, methi, bathua) are richest folate sources
Legumes (moong, masoor, rajma) provide excellent plant folate
Amalaki/Amla — daily consumption recommended
Avoid overcooking vegetables — destroys folate
contraindications: Megaloblastic anemia requires medical folate/B12 supplementation
Pregnant women with folate deficiency need medical folic acid

prehypertension.description: Raktachapa Vriddhi — early blood pressure elevation with Rakta Dhatu and Pitta vitiation

remedies.name: Sarpagandha Vati
remedies.preparation: Herbs powdered and formed into tablets with gum acacia binder
remedies.dosage: 1-2 tablets at bedtime with warm water
remedies.source: Referenced in Charaka Samhita context; classical Ayurvedic pharmacopoeia
remedies.mechanism: Sarpagandha contains reserpine alkaloids — potent antihypertensive; Jatamansi calms nervous system; Shankhapushpi reduces mental stress

remedies.name: Arjuna Ksheerapaka
remedies.preparation: Arjuna bark powder boiled in milk and water (1:8:32) until milk portion remains
remedies.dosage: 1 cup daily in the morning
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana, Ch. 25; Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 26 (Hridroga)
remedies.mechanism: Arjuna is Hridya (cardioprotective) — strengthens cardiac muscle, reduces peripheral resistance; milk medium nourishes Rasa Dhatu

lifestyle: Reduce salt intake — prefer Saindhava (rock salt) in small amounts
Daily walking 30-45 minutes in nature (Vanacharya)
Meditation 20 minutes daily to reduce stress-driven Pitta
Avoid anger, competitive stress — aggravates Pitta and blood pressure
yoga_asanas: Shavasana — deep relaxation reduces blood pressure
Viparita Karani — reduces peripheral vascular resistance
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — most effective pranayama for blood pressure
Sheetali (Cooling Breath) — reduces Pitta
Bhramari — calms sympathetic nervous system
Avoid Bhastrika and Kapalabhati — can raise BP
dietary_principles: DASH-like Ayurvedic approach: fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy
Reduce Lavana (salt) — maximum 1/4 tsp Saindhava per meal
Increase Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa foods
Garlic (Lasuna) — 2 cloves daily, well-established Raktachapa-reducing effect
contraindications: BP consistently > 140/90 requires medical antihypertensive management
Sarpagandha should not be used during pregnancy or with depression

cardiac_risk.description: Hridroga — heart disease risk with Rasa-Rakta Dhatu vitiation and Srotas blockage

remedies.name: Arjuna Churna
remedies.preparation: Dried bark powdered and sieved fine
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm milk or water, twice daily
remedies.source: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana, Ch. 26 (Hridroga Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Arjuna is the foremost Hridya Dravya in Ayurveda — rich in arjunolic acid that strengthens cardiac muscle, reduces LDL oxidation, and improves ejection fraction

remedies.name: Hridayarnava Rasa
remedies.preparation: Classical Rasa preparation with gem/mineral Pishtis triturated with Arjuna decoction
remedies.dosage: 125-250 mg twice daily with honey and Arjuna Ksheerapaka
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya; referenced in cardiac Chikitsa traditions
remedies.mechanism: Calcium from coral and pearl supports cardiac conductivity; Arjuna provides cardioprotection; mineral Bhasmas strengthen Hridaya

remedies.name: Triphala Guggulu
remedies.preparation: Guggulu softened and mixed with Triphala and Pippali powders, formed into tablets
remedies.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with warm water
remedies.source: Sushruta Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana; Sharangdhara Samhita
remedies.mechanism: Guggulsterones lower cholesterol and triglycerides — well-studied lipid-lowering; Triphala provides antioxidant support; Pippali enhances bioavailability

lifestyle: Daily walking 45-60 minutes — gentle cardio per Charaka's exercise guidelines
Manage stress through meditation and Pranayama
Avoid smoking and excess alcohol absolutely
Maintain healthy weight — reduce Meda Dhatu excess
yoga_asanas: Vrikshasana (Tree Pose) — improves balance and heart rate variability
Setubandhasana (Bridge) — opens chest, strengthens heart area
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — balances autonomic nervous system
Bhramari — reduces heart rate and anxiety
Ujjayi — gentle cardiac toning
Avoid vigorous Kapalabhati if BP elevated
dietary_principles: Garlic (Lasuna) 2-3 cloves daily — Hridya and lipid-lowering
Omega-3 from flaxseeds (Atasi) and walnuts (Akhrot)
Reduce Snigdha (oily), Guru (heavy) foods
Include Arjuna bark tea daily as cardiac tonic
contraindications: Acute cardiac events require emergency medical care
Do not discontinue prescribed cardiac medications for Ayurvedic alternatives

vitamin_a_deficiency.description: Netra Roga and Twak Roga — eye and skin disorders from Rasa Dhatu depletion

remedies.name: Triphala Ghrita
remedies.preparation: Triphala decoction used as liquid medium to prepare medicated ghee
remedies.dosage: 1-2 teaspoons with warm water, twice daily
remedies.source: Sushruta Samhita, Uttara Tantra, Ch. 17 (Netra Chikitsa)
remedies.mechanism: Amalaki in Triphala is rich in vitamin A precursors; ghee is the ideal medium for fat-soluble vitamin absorption; classical eye tonic

remedies.name: Shatavari Kalpa
remedies.preparation: Shatavari root powder processed with milk and sugar
remedies.dosage: 5-10 g with warm milk, once daily
remedies.mechanism: Shatavari nourishes Rasa and Rakta Dhatu; supports mucous membrane health; rich in saponins that enhance nutrient absorption

lifestyle: Include orange/yellow vegetables daily (carrots, sweet potato, pumpkin)
Moderate sun exposure for overall Dhatu health
Ghee consumption daily — medium for fat-soluble vitamins
Regular eye exercises (Netra Vyayama)
yoga_asanas: Sarvangasana — improves blood flow to eyes
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — improves Prana to sensory organs
Bhramari with eyes closed — soothes optic nerves
dietary_principles: Ghee — primary fat-soluble vitamin carrier in Ayurveda
Carrot, mango, papaya, drumstick leaves — beta-carotene rich
Liver (for non-vegetarians) — richest preformed vitamin A source
Moringa (Sahijan) leaves — excellent plant source
contraindications: Severe night blindness or keratomalacia requires medical vitamin A supplementation
Pregnant women should not take excess preformed vitamin A

vitamin_e_deficiency.description: Vata Vyadhi — nerve and tissue oxidative damage from Vata-driven Dhatu Kshaya

remedies.name: Ashwagandha Ghrita
remedies.preparation: Ashwagandha root powder boiled in ghee with milk until ghee base remains
remedies.dosage: 1-2 teaspoons daily with warm milk
remedies.mechanism: Ghee provides fat-soluble vitamin medium; Ashwagandha is antioxidant Rasayana; nourishes Majja and Asthi Dhatu

remedies.name: Til Taila (Sesame Oil) Abhyanga
remedies.preparation: Warm sesame oil for external full-body massage
remedies.dosage: Daily Abhyanga before bath — 15-20 minutes
remedies.mechanism: Sesame oil is richest culinary source of vitamin E; transdermal absorption provides antioxidant benefit; pacifies Vata excellently

lifestyle: Daily sesame oil consumption (1-2 tsp) — richest vitamin E food source
Include almonds, sunflower seeds, wheat germ daily
Regular Abhyanga (oil massage) with sesame oil
Adequate dietary fat to ensure fat-soluble vitamin absorption
yoga_asanas: Matsyasana (Fish Pose) — opens chest, improves antioxidant circulation
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — balances Vata and improves tissue oxygenation
Ujjayi — gentle warming pranayama
dietary_principles: Sesame seeds and oil (Til) — Ayurveda's premier Vata-pacifying fat, richest in vitamin E
Almonds (Badam) — soaked and peeled, 8-10 daily
Wheat germ, sunflower seeds, avocado
Ghee in diet ensures absorption of all fat-soluble vitamins
contraindications: Peripheral neuropathy from vitamin E deficiency may need medical supplementation
Do not follow very low-fat diets — impairs fat-soluble vitamin absorption

zinc_deficiency.description: Ojas Kshaya — depletion of vital essence affecting immunity and tissue repair

remedies.name: Yashada Bhasma
remedies.preparation: Zinc purified (Shodhana) and incinerated (Marana) with Aloe vera juice per Rasa Shastra methodology
remedies.dosage: 125-250 mg with honey, twice daily
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya; referenced in Charaka Samhita mineral therapeutics
remedies.mechanism: Bio-available zinc from Bhasma preparation; supports immune function (Vyadhikshamatva) and tissue healing (Dhatu Poshana)

remedies.name: Shatavari Churna
remedies.preparation: Root powder dried and sieved
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g with warm milk and honey, twice daily
remedies.mechanism: Shatavari is Rasayana — nourishes Rasa Dhatu and supports zinc absorption; immunomodulatory saponins boost Ojas

lifestyle: Include pumpkin seeds (Kaddu ke beej) — richest zinc food source
Soak legumes before cooking to reduce phytates that block zinc
Regular sleep for immune recovery and Dhatu repair
Avoid excess phytate-rich foods with zinc-rich meals
pranayama: Kapalabhati — boosts immune function
Bhastrika — energizing for immune system
Nadi Shodhana — balances Prana for healing
dietary_principles: Pumpkin seeds, chickpeas, lentils, meat, cashews — zinc-rich foods
Soak and sprout legumes to reduce phytic acid
Yogurt — fermentation reduces phytates, zinc more available
Avoid excess coffee/tea with zinc-rich meals
contraindications: Severe zinc deficiency with impaired wound healing needs medical supplementation
Yashada Bhasma should only be from certified Ayurvedic manufacturers

calcium_magnesium_deficiency.description: Asthi-Majja Dhatu Kshaya — bone and marrow tissue depletion leading to osteopenia risk

remedies.name: Praval Pishti
remedies.preparation: Coral triturated with rose water for 21 days until extremely fine
remedies.dosage: 250-500 mg with honey, twice daily
remedies.source: Rasa Ratna Samucchaya; referenced in Sushruta Samhita mineral therapeutics
remedies.mechanism: Coral provides bio-available calcium carbonate; rose water processing enhances Pitta-pacifying quality; directly nourishes Asthi Dhatu

remedies.name: Kukkutandatwak Bhasma
remedies.preparation: Eggshells cleaned, incinerated (Marana) with lemon juice cycles per Rasa Shastra
remedies.dosage: 250-500 mg with warm milk, twice daily
remedies.source: Referenced in classical Ayurvedic pharmacy texts under Bhasma preparations
remedies.mechanism: Eggshell is 95% calcium carbonate; Bhasma processing makes it nano-particulate and bio-available; acidic medium of lemon enhances calcium release

remedies.name: Ashwagandha Churna with Milk
remedies.preparation: Ashwagandha powder mixed with warm milk and a teaspoon of ghee
remedies.dosage: 3-5 g at bedtime with milk
remedies.mechanism: Ashwagandha increases bone mineral density (modern research confirms); milk provides calcium and magnesium; ghee aids absorption; Rasayana for Asthi Dhatu

lifestyle: Morning sun exposure 15-20 minutes for vitamin D synthesis (aids calcium absorption)
Weight-bearing exercise — walking, stair climbing
Sesame oil Abhyanga — transdermal mineral support
Avoid excess caffeine — increases calcium excretion
yoga_asanas: Vrikshasana (Tree Pose) — weight-bearing balance for bones
Virabhadrasana (Warrior Poses) — strengthens bones and joints
Tadasana — improves posture and spinal bone density
Utkatasana (Chair Pose) — weight-bearing for legs and spine
pranayama: Nadi Shodhana — balances Vata (Vata aggravation drives bone loss)
Deep Breathing — improves oxygen delivery to bone marrow
dietary_principles: Dairy (milk, curd, paneer) — primary calcium sources in Ayurveda
Sesame seeds (Til) — Ayurveda's richest plant calcium source
Dark leafy greens (ragi/nachni is calcium-rich)
Ragi (finger millet) — exceptionally rich in calcium, traditional Ayurvedic grain
contraindications: Osteoporosis with fracture risk needs medical calcium + vitamin D supplementation
Kidney stone patients should consult before calcium supplementation
